stack: Android APK (decompiled) — minSdk 21 / targetSdk 22, no obfuscation, no RASP — the wide-open floor of the mobile ladder

vulns.desc: android:debuggable="true" ships in the application manifest, so any user can attach a JDWP debugger to the running process and read/modify its memory and data on any device.
vulns.exploit: grep android:debuggable AndroidManifest.xml -> true; on device: jdb / run-as without needing a custom-built debug APK.

vulns.desc: android:allowBackup="true" lets the app's private data (/data/data sandbox: SharedPrefs, SQLite) be extracted with `adb backup` on a non-rooted device, and restored/tampered.
vulns.exploit: adb backup -f lv.ab com.leakyvault.app ; abe unpack lv.ab -> read cleartext prefs/db.

vulns.desc: AdminActivity, SyncReceiver and NotesProvider are android:exported="true" with NO permission guard, so any other installed app can launch the internal admin screen, broadcast to the receiver, and query the content provider.
vulns.exploit: am start -n com.leakyvault.app/.AdminActivity ; am broadcast -a com.leakyvault.app.SYNC ; content query --uri content://com.leakyvault.app.notes

vulns.desc: usesCleartextTraffic="true" plus a network_security_config base-config with cleartextTrafficPermitted="true" (and trust-anchors including the user store) means all API traffic runs over plaintext http:// and trusts user-installed CAs — trivially intercepted/MITM'd.
vulns.exploit: grep usesCleartextTraffic / res/xml/network_security_config.xml ; strings show http:// base_url; intercept with any proxy.

vulns.desc: A live-shaped backend API key and an HMAC signing secret are hardcoded in res/values/strings.xml, in assets/config.json, and compiled into the dex (smali ApiClient) — anyone unpacking the APK recovers them.
vulns.exploit: unzip apk ; grep -rEi 'api_key|hmac|secret|lv_live' res/ assets/ smali/ -> lv_live_... + signing secret.
